solute.description: This molecule consists of two para-substituted phenyl rings linked by an ethylamide chain, with one ring bearing a terminal 2-methylpropanoic acid group attached via an ether bond. Distinctive functional groups include a carboxylic acid, a secondary amide, a para-chloro substituent, and a sterically hindered quaternary carbon at the alpha position of the acid. The structure exhibits significant polarity and hydrogen bonding capacity through its acid and amide moieties, while the chlorinated aromatic rings and gem-dimethyl group contribute to its overall size and hydrophobic character.

solvent.description: This molecule is a five-carbon branched aliphatic alcohol consisting of a four-carbon primary chain with a methyl substituent at the C3 position, forming an isopentyl skeleton. It features a single primary hydroxyl group attached to the terminal carbon (C1). The structure is characterized by a small molecular size and an amphiphilic nature, possessing a hydrophobic branched alkyl tail and a polar head capable of acting as both a hydrogen bond donor and acceptor.
